name: Baryon asymmetry of the universe
summary: The observed baryon-to-photon ratio eta = n_B / n_gamma ~ 6e-10 (from BBN + CMB) requires a CP-violating, baryon-number-violating, out-of-equilibrium process in the early universe (Sakharov 1967). The SM has all three Sakharov conditions in principle (CKM CP violation, sphaleron B+L violation, electroweak phase transition) but the CP violation is too small by ~10 orders of magnitude and the EW transition is a crossover, so the SM cannot produce the observed asymmetry.

characteristic_numbers.note: Both naive expectations are excluded; the observed small but non-zero value requires an out-of-equilibrium CP-violating mechanism beyond the SM.

characteristic_numbers.expected: needed > 1e-5 if SM-like processes drove BAU
characteristic_numbers.note: Even if the EW transition were strongly first-order (it isn't), CKM CP violation is ~10 orders of magnitude too weak.

principles_stressed.note: BAU forces additional sources of CP violation beyond CKM, motivating heavy-Majorana CP phases (leptogenesis), supersymmetric phases, or extended scalar sectors. (Listed under 'cpt' as the closest enum value; the puzzle stresses C and CP violation, not CPT itself.) EDM bounds constrain these strongly.

history: Sakharov conditions formulated 1967. SM electroweak baryogenesis investigated thoroughly through the 1990s and ruled out (Higgs too heavy, EW transition is crossover). Leptogenesis (Fukugita-Yanagida 1986) emerged as the leading framework, building on the seesaw. Affleck-Dine baryogenesis is a SUSY-friendly alternative.

references.citation: Sakharov, 'Violation of CP invariance, C asymmetry, and baryon asymmetry of the universe', JETP Lett. 5, 24 (1967)
Fukugita and Yanagida, 'Baryogenesis without grand unification', Phys. Lett. B 174, 45 (1986)
